bibliographic_data.title: Visual Self-Refinement for Autoregressive Models
bibliographic_data.abstract: Autoregressive models excel in sequential modeling and have proven to be effective for vision-language data. However, the spatial nature of visual signals conflicts with the sequential dependencies of next-token prediction, leading to suboptimal results. This work proposes a plug-and-play refinement module to enhance the complex spatial correspondence modeling within the generated visual sequence. This module operates as a post-pretraining step to jointly refine all generated tokens of autoregressive model, enhancing vision-language modeling under a shared sequential prediction framework. By leveraging global context and relationships across the tokens, our method mitigates the error accumulation issue within the sequential generation. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method improves the generation quality, enhancing the model’s ability to produce semantically consistent results.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper introduces a post-pretraining finetuning operation that enhances autoregressive models for visual data without harming the generalizeability of the pretrained model.
core_content.key_methodology: A plug-and-play self-refinement module is proposed to operate on generated tokens while keeping the pretrained backbone frozen. The module jointly optimizes the generated tokens under supervision from the target image, mapping discrete tokens to an embedding space to facilitate learning and consistent corrections across the sequence.
outcomes: Enhanced model’s ability to produce semantically consistent results
core_content.full_text_markdown: Autoregressive (AR) models have achieved remarkable success in recent years, particularly in multimodal tasks. However, when applied to visual data, the spatial nature of images conflicts with the sequential dependencies required for next-token prediction, leading to suboptimal results. This paper proposes a plug-and-play self-refinement module that operates as a post-pretraining step to enhance the complex spatial correspondence modeling within generated visual sequences. The method jointly refines all tokens of an autoregressive model under supervision from the target image, leveraging global context and relationships across tokens to mitigate error accumulation in sequential generation. Experiments demonstrate significant improvements in generation quality, enhancing the model's ability to produce semantically consistent results.
